query: Latest news June-July 2026 on Johor state election campaign, expected voter turnout projections, analyst forecasts, and any factors affecting turnout like Singapore commuter voters. What turnout numbers are analysts and Election Commission SPR predicting for July 11 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the Johor July 11, 2026 state election turnout:

**Bottom line on turnout projections:** There is **no publicly reported official SPR/Election Commission turnout forecast** — only academic/political analyst projections. The consensus is that turnout will **rise above the 2022 record low (54.92%) but most likely land in the 60–70% range**, with several analysts saying it will stay below 65%.

**Specific analyst forecasts:**
- UM's Tawfik Yaakub: higher than 2022, but likely below 65%
- UTM's Mazlan Ali: 65–70% (up to 70% with incentives); crucially, <60% favors BN, while 75–80% could let PH take Johor
- UTHM's Md Akbal Abdullah: at least 60%
- Straits Times summary: "likely remain below 65 per cent"

**Key context:**
- 2022 turnout was a historic low 54.92% (only 1.43M ballots), depressed by COVID border closures. 2018 was 84.51%.
- Electorate: ~2.73 million; ~47.6% under age 40 (Undi18 effect).

**The Singapore commuter factor:** ~400,000 Johoreans cross the Causeway daily, and ~300,000 young Johor voters live outside the state (many in Singapore). Their absence in 2022 was the main cause of the low turnout. For 2026, PH has arranged **extra KL–Johor trains and expedited customs/immigration lanes**, and the Saturday weekend polling date should help mobilize this bloc — the single biggest swing factor for turnout. The full report, data points, and sources have been recorded in the structured output.

key_facts: Johor state election is scheduled for Saturday, July 11, 2026, contesting all 56 state assembly seats; nomination day was June 27, advance voting July 7.
Registered voters: 2,727,926 as of May 31, 2026 (~2.73 million); one report cited 2,725,559.
2022 Johor state election turnout was a historic-low 54.92% (1,426,573 ballots), depressed by COVID border closures; 2018 turnout was 84.51%.
No official SPR/Election Commission turnout prediction has been publicly reported; forecasts come from academic/political analysts.
Analyst consensus: turnout will rise above the 2022 low but most expect it to remain in the 60-70% range, with several saying likely below 65%.
UTM's Mazlan Ali projects 65-70% (up to 70% with incentives) and says <60% turnout favors BN while 75-80% could let PH take Johor.
UM's Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub expects higher than 2022 but likely below 65%; UTHM's Md Akbal Abdullah expects at least 60%.
~47.6% of Johor's electorate (~1.29-1.3 million) is under 40 due to Undi18 and automatic registration; seen as decisive swing bloc.
About 300,000 young Johor voters live outside the state (many in Singapore); ~400,000 Johoreans cross the Causeway to Singapore daily.
PH arranged additional KL-Johor trains and expedited customs/immigration lanes to mobilize Singapore-based/outstation voters to return and vote.
EC issued 24,677 postal ballots; election budget is RM167.4 million (~US$41.2 million); campaign period is 14 days.
Main contest is BN (incumbent, Onn Hafiz Ghazi) vs PH; Perikatan Nasional is notably weaker in Johor; PAS fielded 11 candidates; MUDA's Syed Saddiq is sitting out the campaign.
